text.title: SDD and Living Documentation Trends 2025-2026
text.summary: SDD as dominant paradigm for AI-assisted development, docs-as-code patterns, LLM-optimized documentation, and developer portal landscape.

section.title: Specification-Driven Development

rule.statement: Treat the specification as the source of truth; code is its output, not the input to understanding.
rule.rationale: SDD emerged as the dominant paradigm for AI-assisted development in 2025. The core principle — intent is the source of truth — reverses the traditional relationship where code is read to understand intent.

section.p: Core workflow: Intent (human) → Spec (human + LLM draft) → Plan (LLM + human review) → Execute (LLM) → Review (human).

list.item: Feature work in existing systems (N to N+1): highest effectiveness
Vibe coding / rapid prototypes: low (use different approach)

section.p: Key SDD tools as of 2025: Amazon Kiro (full SDD workflow with hooks), Spec Kit (GitHub/Microsoft open-source), Tessl (framework and registry), Claude Code (native SDD support).

section.title: Living Documentation

rule.statement: Treat documentation as first-class code: version-controlled, CI/CD pipelines, automated testing.
rule.rationale: Documentation that is not in version control becomes stale immediately. Docs-as-code enables the same quality gates applied to code to be applied to documentation.

section.p: Docs-as-code core stack: Markdown/MDX format, Git storage, static site generators (Docusaurus, MkDocs, Mintlify), automated linting and link checking in CI/CD.

list.item: Clear heading hierarchy: better LLM parsing
Code blocks with language tags: accurate code generation
API contracts in specs: type-safe integration

section.title: Developer Portal Landscape
section.p: Developer portals are UI; platforms are the backend. The portal is not the platform.

list.item: Backstage (89% market share): large orgs, extensibility, TechDocs integration
Port (rising): service catalogs, workflows, REST API for agent registration
Roadie (SaaS leader): Backstage without ops overhead
Mintlify: llms.txt standard; Anthropic uses for Claude docs

rule.statement: Load this trends document at the start of quarterly roadmap sessions; do not load it per-task.
rule.rationale: Trend documents are orientation resources, not per-task guides. Loading per-task wastes context tokens and produces over-confident recommendations based on trend data that should be treated as directional.
